'''''Galaxy of Fear: Army of Terror''''' is the sixth book in the ''[[Star Wars: Galaxy of Fear]]'' series.

==Publisher's summary==
''Tash, Zak, and their uncle Hoole want to make sure that Borborygmus Gog's Project Starscream has been destroyed. So they journey to Kiva, a planet on the galaxy's Outer Rim''.

''Kiva is dark, rocky, and barren.''

''Except for some strange shadows, the planet is completely lifeless.''

''And when they find Gog's laboratory, it's empty. Or so they think….''

==Plot summary==
[[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] all make an appearance in the book as they attempt to stop [[Project Starscream]]. Their appearance in the book is apparently prior to their relocation to [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]].

The [[Kivan|shadow creatures]] inhabiting [[Kiva]] were reduced to that state by an accident caused by [[Borborygmus Gog|Gog]] and [[Mammon Hoole|Hoole]]. Gog's [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]] is disguised as an innocent [[Youngling/Legends|baby]], with a bruise on his forehead, named "[[Eppon]]". Many people left alone with the baby mysteriously disappear. The baby also grows at an alarming rate and quickly matures into an adult. When the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] find an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Laboratory/Legends|lab]], they also find Gog. Gog reveals that Hoole's full name is Mammon Hoole, who the Shadows had been talking about. Then, it is revealed that Eppon is really an Imperial [[Bioweapon/Legends|Bio-weapon]]. It then transforms into a giant monster, and tries to eat Hoole. Tash convinces Eppon to turn on Gog, who promptly [[Explosive charge/Legends|blows]] Eppon up with a remote control he had in his [[Jacket/Legends|coat]], much to Tash's dismay. Suddenly, the Shadows burst in to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Hoole, when suddenly the [[Computer/Legends|computer]] in the lab is switched on, revealing that it was Gog who rigged an experiment to make the power of a [[Star/Legends|star]] in a test tube fail, creating the Shadows. The shadows promptly kill Gog for that.
